output:
Implemented T1 in `useTimelineSave.ts`: added `isSavingRef`/`pendingSaveRef`, guarded overlapping `saveTimeline` calls, and drained the latest queued save from `finally` using the pending save's own `seq`. Verified with targeted file inspection and `npx eslint src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineSave.ts`.

Best-effort checkpointing was not written because `.megaplan/plans/light-megaplan-fix-the-20260327-0056/execution_batch_1.json` was not writable.

task_updates.executor_notes: Added serialized save guard in `saveTimeline`: `isSavingRef.current` is set before async mutation work, overlapping calls now stash `{ data, seq }` into `pendingSaveRef`, and `finally` resets `isSavingRef`, clears `pendingSaveRef` before draining, then re-invokes `saveTimeline` with the pending save's own `seq`. Verified by code inspection and passing targeted ESLint on `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineSave.ts`.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Verified the serialization guard behavior in `useTimelineSave.ts`: (a) `isSavingRef.current = true` is set before `mutateAsync` and reset in `finally`; (b) `pendingSaveRef.current` is nulled before the drain call; (c) the drain path calls `saveTimeline(pendingSave.data, pendingSave.seq)`, so it uses the queued save's `seq` rather than `editSeqRef.current`.
